mnemonics: PATRICIA: Practical Algorithm To Retrieve Information Coded In Alphanumeric - Helps remember the full form
M P T: Merkle Patricia Tree - Three types of nodes: Branch, Leaf, Extension, and Empty (B.L.E.E.)
For Patricia compression: SINGLE CHILD? COMPRESS IT! - Remember when to apply path compression
For Ethereum MPT: 16 children for Branch (0-F hex) - Like having 16 fingers to count with

acronyms.expansion: Practical Algorithm to Retrieve Information Coded in Alphanumeric
acronyms.usage: The original acronym coined by Donald R. Morrison, describing the purpose of efficient alphanumeric data retrieval
Ethereum's data structure combining Merkle hashes with Patricia tree structure for state management
The compression technique used in Patricia trees, where consecutive single-child nodes are merged
Ethereum's key encoding unit; each hex character represents one nibble (4 bits)

visualTips: Think of Patricia tree as a highway with express lanes: single-child paths are 'express lanes' skipping multiple exits (nodes)
Imagine a tree branch that splits only once at the end - Patricia tree merges this into one thick branch
For MPT: Think of each node as a box with a lock (hash) - changing anything inside changes the lock number
Branch node = intersection with up to 16 roads (hex digits 0-F) plus one parking spot (value storage)

analogies.analogy: Like taking a direct flight instead of hopping through multiple layovers - you skip the intermediate stops
Like a digital fingerprint of the entire database - any change creates a completely different fingerprint
Trie is like spelling out each letter of a word on separate cards; Patricia groups consecutive single-letter cards into one card with the full word segment
Like verifying a bank statement by checking only the final balance and a summary hash, without reviewing every single transaction individually
